question_text: A student investigates four different forces acting on objects: gravitational force on a falling ball, elastic force in a stretched spring, normal force on a book on a table, and magnetic force between two magnets. Which of these forces can act on an object WITHOUT the two objects being in direct contact?

options.label: A
options.text: Only the elastic force and normal force

options.label: B
options.text: Only the gravitational force and magnetic force

options.label: C
options.text: Only the normal force and elastic force

options.label: D
options.text: All four forces can act without direct contact

explanation: Gravitational force and magnetic force are non-contact forces that can act across a distance without objects touching. The elastic force requires a spring to be stretched (contact), and normal force requires objects to be in contact with a surface. This question requires students to classify and compare force types based on their fundamental properties.
